title: salix commutata
intro: Salix commutata, also known as the prickly willow or blue-leaved willow, is a deciduous shrub or small tree native to the western regions of North America. The plant's bark contains salicylic acid, a compound similar to aspirin, which has been used for centuries in herbal medicine for its anti-inflammatory and pain-relieving properties. The medicinal properties of Salix commutata have been traditionally used to treat various health issues, including fever, rheumatism, and digestive problems, offering relief from pain and inflammation. In herbal preparations, the bark of the Salix commutata plant is often used to make teas, tinctures, and salves, which can be consumed or applied topically to harness its medicinal benefits.
